channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Edmonton Oilers Extend Colton Dach's Contract for Two Years
item.description: The Edmonton Oilers have signed forward Colton Dach to a two-year contract extension with an average annual value of US$1.2 million, according to the NHL club. Dach, acquired from the Chicago Blackhaw...

item.title: U.S. Citizen in Congo Tests Positive for Ebola as Outbreak Escalates
item.description: A U.S. citizen working for a humanitarian organization in the Democratic Republic of Congo has tested positive for the Ebola virus, according to the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC). T...

item.title: Habagat rains expected to bring scattered showers over Metro Manila and Luzon
item.description: The southwest monsoon, known as habagat, is expected to continue bringing scattered rains over Metro Manila and several parts of Luzon. According to the Philippine Atmospheric, Geophysical and Astrono...

item.title: Former Defence Spokesman Enenche Hails Rescue Of Oyo Abductees, Calls For Closing Security Gaps
item.description: Major General John Enenche (retd), former Director of Media Operations at the Defence Headquarters, praised security agencies and the government for rescuing over 50 abducted Oyo students and teachers...

item.title: Police investigate motive behind Ann Widdecombe's murder following suspect arrest
item.description: Devon and Cornwall Police have stated they are keeping an open mind regarding the motive behind the murder of veteran politician Ann Widdecombe, following the arrest of a 28-year-old suspect in Rother...

item.title: Ghalib Said Mohamed's Business Empire and Family Legacy in East Africa
item.description: The article profiles Ghalib Said Mohamed, a Tanzanian entrepreneur who transformed his father's cashew-trading business into the diversified GSM Group. Leading the conglomerate, he oversees operations...

item.title: Deep ocean turbulence influences global climate patterns
item.description: Research published in Nature Communications reveals that tiny waves in the deep ocean, previously thought to only impact over long timescales, can significantly affect surface conditions within a sing...

item.title: NSFAS administrator defends R10m adviser appointments amid EFF calls for investigation
item.description: Professor Hlengani Mathebula, administrator of the National Student Financial Aid Scheme (NSFAS), has denied allegations that he appointed four technical advisers at an annual cost of R10 million. Mat...

item.title: Five Punjab Workers Killed by Terrorists Near Pakistan-Iran Border, PM Condemns Attack
item.description: Five workers from Punjab were killed in a terrorist attack near the Pakistan-Iran border, according to a recent report. The incident has sparked strong reactions from the government, with Prime Minist...

item.title: Sari-sari Store Owner's Cellphone Stolen by Neighbor in Tondo
item.description: A sari-sari store owner in Tondo, Manila, reported that their cellphone was stolen by a neighbor. The incident, which occurred on July 13, 2026, highlights the growing issue of petty theft in resident...

item.title: Political commentators criticize Supreme Court's handling of Trump's birthright citizenship case
item.description: The article reports on reactions from political commentator David Pakman and independent journalist Katie Phang following a recent Supreme Court decision involving President Donald Trump and birthrigh...

item.title: U.S. and Iran Clash Over Strait of Hormuz Status Amid Rising Tensions
item.description: The United States and Iran are locked in a bitter dispute over the status of the Strait of Hormuz, a critical waterway through which a significant portion of the world's oil shipments pass. The U.S. m...

item.title: Wildfires in Wales and Across UK Trigger Major Incident Response
item.description: A major incident has been declared in north Wales following a large-scale wildfire that has prompted evacuations and emergency responses across the UK. Firefighters are working under challenging condi...

item.title: Venezuela's Economic Crisis Persists Despite US Intervention
item.description: Six months after the US raid that captured Venezuelan President Nicolás Maduro, locals report little improvement in the country's dire economic situation. Despite claims by former President Donald Tru...

item.title: Rising CO₂ levels threaten large bees and disrupt pollinator ecosystems
item.description: Pollinators like bees, hoverflies, and birds are vital for maintaining healthy ecosystems and food production. However, climate change, particularly rising carbon dioxide levels, poses a significant t...

item.title: Behind-the-Scenes Drama Between Gabo and Victor in Coco Martin’s Sigabo
item.description: The article provides an exclusive look at the intense on-set interactions between actors Gabo Magtibay and Victor Tigre Zafra during filming of Coco Martin’s television series 'Sigabo'. The behind-the...

item.title: Minister Umahi Confirms Death Of Staff Member At His Residence, Calls For Autopsy
item.description: David Umahi, Nigeria’s Works Minister, confirmed that Mary Habila, a physiotherapist seconded to his residence from the David Umahi Federal University of Health Sciences, died at his Uburu home. He re...

item.title: Waterspout Spotted Off Prince Edward Island Coast: Islanders Experience Rare Natural Phenomenon
item.description: Laura MacNeil, a seasoned shoreline tour guide in P.E.I., and her group witnessed a rare waterspout off the Vernon Bridge area on Saturday morning. The event, confirmed by the Northern Tornadoes Proje...

item.title: Madlanga Commission Highlights Gaps in Public Servant Financial Disclosure System
item.description: The Madlanga Commission has uncovered significant flaws in South Africa's financial disclosure system for public servants, according to testimony by Dr Albertus Schoeman, a World Bank anti-corruption ...

item.title: US and Allies Reaffirm Commitment to 2016 South China Sea Tribunal Ruling
item.description: The United States Embassy in the Philippines, along with Australia, Canada, Estonia, Germany, Italy, Japan, Latvia, Lithuania, New Zealand, Romania, Slovenia, and the United Kingdom, jointly reaffirme...
